summary: Matched the lipstick item source by adding its ItemStateTable definition in src/melee/it/items/itlipstick.c, adding the forward typedef include needed for ItemStateTable, correcting one GALE01 sdata2 symbol from a global it_804DCB68 name to the local @158 label, and flipping itlipstick.c from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py.
what_changed: Added #include "it/items/forward.h" to itlipstick.c before defining an ItemStateTable.

Defined ItemStateTable it_803F6310[] with six entries, wiring the lipstick motion states to existing itLipstick_UnkMotion*_Anim, *_Phys, and *_Coll callbacks, including one NULL collision callback for motion 2.
Updated configure.py so Object(NonMatching, "melee/it/items/itlipstick.c") became Object(Matching, "melee/it/items/itlipstick.c").
Changed the symbol at .sdata2:0x804DCB68 in config/GALE01/symbols.txt from global it_804DCB68 to local @158 while preserving type, size, alignment, and float data annotation.
smart_moves: The matching fix was small and targeted: reconstruct the item state table in C instead of disturbing the already-existing lipstick behavior functions.
The added forward.h include supplies the ItemStateTable type without introducing broader item-item header dependencies in the diff.
The symbol-map update recognizes that the 0x804DCB68 float should be treated as a compiler-style local @ label rather than a named global, reducing symbol-scope mismatch risk.
The PR paired the source/data match with the configure.py status flip, making the build system reflect the new matching state immediately.
decomp_lessons: For item object matches, missing ItemStateTable data can be the remaining blocker even when the callback functions already exist and match.
When reconstructing item state tables, preserve the exact callback ordering, flag values, and NULL entries; the table layout is part of the match.
Matching may require symbol-scope corrections in config/GALE01/symbols.txt, not only C source edits.
After an object matches, remember to move it from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py so future builds enforce the match.
naming_conventions: Lipstick callbacks use the project’s item naming pattern: itLipstick_UnkMotionN_Anim, itLipstick_UnkMotionN_Phys, and itLipstick_UnkMotionN_Coll.
The item state table keeps an address-based name, it_803F6310, because no semantic table name was introduced in this PR.
Compiler-local sdata symbols may be represented with @NNN labels, as shown by changing it_804DCB68 to @158.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Materialize the rodata/data table in C with exact ItemStateTable entries to satisfy the object match.
Use NULL explicitly in table slots where the original callback pointer is absent.
Adjust local/global symbol treatment in symbols.txt when the disassembly indicates a local float constant rather than a reusable global.
Only after source and symbol changes are in place, mark the object Matching in configure.py.
review_feedback: No PR body, issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the slice; there is no explicit reviewer feedback to extract.
follow_up_queries: Find other item files still marked Object(NonMatching) in configure.py that may only need an ItemStateTable definition.
Search symbols.txt for remaining it_804D* float globals near item code that should be compiler-local @ labels.
Compare other matched item state tables for callback ordering and flag patterns reusable in remaining item matches.
